videos.title: Should My AI Coding Agent be Open Source?
videos.summary_source_text:
Should My AI Coding Agent be Open Source? Join the community: https://mrc.fm/cmc

I built Tank to run my own AI coding agents and it has taken on a life of its own.
So today I am going live to clear up what Tank actually is, and to talk about
something a lot of you have been asking for.

Quick version: Tank is mission control for AI coding agents. You give an agent a
task, it runs in a real terminal you can watch and jump into, with live feedback, a
build queue for overnight work, and scheduled jobs. A human stays in the loop the
whole way. It is not auto merge and it is not a black box. It is your command centre
for Claude Code, Codex, Grok and more.

Now the big one. A lot of you have asked me to open it up so you can run the code
yourself. I have been seriously exploring exactly that: an open, source available
core you could try, self host and build on, with the advanced features as optional
modules. Today I want to tease where this is heading and hear from you.

So come and tell me: would you run Tank yourself? Would you build a module? Should
the core be open? Bring your questions, I am answering live.

Get early access and help shape what happens next: https://mrc.fm/cmc

00:00 Intro: What is Tank?
01:03 The Open Source Question
01:58 When AI Agents Take Over Your Socials
03:52 Tour of the Tank UI
04:14 What Problem Does Tank Solve?
07:08 Live Demo: Spawning Tasks & Servers
11:04 Clarifying the Focus (Tank vs. Auto PR Mergers)
12:10 How Tank Differs from Cursor & Hermes
13:44 Managing API Costs & Rotating AI Models
14:55 Why a Product Model Over Community-Only?
16:35 Future Vision: Plugins & Marketplace Add-Ons
22:47 Multi-Agent Architecture to Prevent Bugs
25:52 Discussing the "Open Core" Model
30:10 Managing Rapid Community Growth
32:21 Introducing the Core Team & Outro Join the community: https://mrc.fm/cmc

videos.title: Scottie Scheffler goes low on Thursday | Round 1 | Travelers Championship | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
Scottie Scheffler goes low on Thursday | Round 1 | Travelers Championship | 2026 In the opening round of the 2026 Travelers Championship, Scottie Scheffler shoots a 6-under, bogey-free 64, and sits T2 on the leaderboard.

The PGA TOUR’s final Signature Event of the 2026 season takes center stage this week at the Travelers Championship, where many of the game’s biggest stars make the short trip from Shinnecock Hills to TPC River Highlands. Fresh off his U.S. Open victory, Wyndham Clark arrives in Connecticut looking to continue a remarkable run that has included two victories in his last four starts, while fellow major contenders seek to carry their momentum into one of the TOUR’s most popular events. World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler headlines a stacked field after finishing T4 at the U.S. Open and returns to a venue where he has recorded three consecutive top-six finishes, including a playoff victory in 2024. Also in action are Cameron Young, Matt Fitzpatrick and Russell Henley, while eight of the world’s top 10 players are set to compete at TPC River Highlands. Defending champion Keegan Bradley returns looking to become the first player since 2002 to successfully defend the Travelers title. Bradley has won the event twice in the last three years, while former champions Harris English, Xander Schauffele and Jordan Spieth are also in the field. Recent form players Sam Burns and J.T. Poston arrive with plenty of confidence after strong performances during the major championship stretch, while Jason Day is expected to tee it up despite withdrawing from the U.S. Open with a back injury. With 700 FedExCup points available and the final Signature Event trophy of the season on the line, the Travelers Championship once again features one of the strongest fields of the year. Played at the fan-favorite TPC River Highlands, one of the shortest courses on the PGA TOUR schedule, the event is set for another exciting week highlighted by elite talent and plenty of summer momentum.

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 1 | Travelers Championship | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 1 | Travelers Championship | 2026 Check out all the best shots from the first round of the Travelers Championship, featuring Scottie Scheffler, Ben Griffin, Matt Fitzpatrick, among others.

The PGA TOUR’s final Signature Event of the 2026 season takes center stage this week at the Travelers Championship, where many of the game’s biggest stars make the short trip from Shinnecock Hills to TPC River Highlands. Fresh off his U.S. Open victory, Wyndham Clark arrives in Connecticut looking to continue a remarkable run that has included two victories in his last four starts, while fellow major contenders seek to carry their momentum into one of the TOUR’s most popular events. World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler headlines a stacked field after finishing T4 at the U.S. Open and returns to a venue where he has recorded three consecutive top-six finishes, including a playoff victory in 2024. Also in action are Cameron Young, Matt Fitzpatrick and Russell Henley, while eight of the world’s top 10 players are set to compete at TPC River Highlands. Defending champion Keegan Bradley returns looking to become the first player since 2002 to successfully defend the Travelers title. Bradley has won the event twice in the last three years, while former champions Harris English, Xander Schauffele and Jordan Spieth are also in the field. Recent form players Sam Burns and J.T. Poston arrive with plenty of confidence after strong performances during the major championship stretch, while Jason Day is expected to tee it up despite withdrawing from the U.S. Open with a back injury. With 700 FedExCup points available and the final Signature Event trophy of the season on the line, the Travelers Championship once again features one of the strongest fields of the year. Played at the fan-favorite TPC River Highlands, one of the shortest courses on the PGA TOUR schedule, the event is set for another exciting week highlighted by elite talent and plenty of summer momentum.

videos.title: Is It Wrong To Use A Snapshot Here?
videos.summary_source_text:
Is It Wrong To Use A Snapshot Here? The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: They're Lying About AI "Brain Rot"
videos.summary_source_text:
They're Lying About AI "Brain Rot" Here's the truth about that MIT AI study everyone's been referencing.

Yes, leaning on AI too early or too completely can make you "dumber." But the full study reveals a much more nuanced reality: human skill + AI performs better than human skill alone.

When you already know how to think critically, AI becomes a superpower. When you use it to replace all critical thinking, well... you may lose the ability to think for yourself 🤷‍♂️

What's another AI claim you keep hearing about that's not entirely true, or more complicated than people are saying?

videos.title: 15 Years of Driver Development... TaylorMade Should Be Panicking
videos.summary_source_text:
15 Years of Driver Development... TaylorMade Should Be Panicking TaylorMade claims its latest driver technology can help golfers gain up to 15 more yards off the tee but is that really true?

In this video, I put 15 years of driver development to the test by comparing a modern TaylorMade driver against one of the most iconic drivers ever made, the TaylorMade R11. With all the advancements in driver technology, forgiveness, ball speed, aerodynamics, carbon construction, and AI face design, you would expect a huge difference in performance.

But the results might surprise you.

If you're looking for the best driver for distance, wondering whether new driver technology is worth the upgrade, or trying to find out if modern drivers really hit the ball further, this test reveals some eye opening truths. We analyse ball speed, carry distance, total distance, forgiveness, launch conditions, spin rates, and consistency to see whether today's drivers genuinely deliver more performance.

In this TaylorMade driver review, you'll discover:

Does a new driver really give you 15 more yards? How much has driver technology improved in 15 years? TaylorMade R11 vs Modern TaylorMade Driver, Best golf drivers for distance in 2026, Are premium driver upgrades worth the money? Driver forgiveness, ball speed and launch monitor data, What golfers should know before buying a new driver

Whether you're a beginner golfer, mid handicap player, or low handicap golfer searching for more distance and accuracy off the tee, this comparison will help you make a smarter buying decision.

Looking for the best golf driver? Want more distance without changing your swing? Curious if older golf clubs can compete with today's technology? Watch until the end to see the full results.

videos.title: JUST A PINCH OF THIS IN YOUR BREAKFAST CAN CHANGE YOUR ENTIRE MORNING | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
JUST A PINCH OF THIS IN YOUR BREAKFAST CAN CHANGE YOUR ENTIRE MORNING | Dr. Mandell Could one of the simplest ingredients in your kitchen help support healthier mornings? In this video, Dr. Alan Mandell explains the science behind adding just a pinch of cinnamon to your breakfast and why it may help support healthy blood sugar levels, improve insulin sensitivity, reduce mid-morning cravings, and provide steadier energy throughout the day. Learn how cinnamon's powerful antioxidants and natural plant compounds work inside the body, the difference between Ceylon and Cassia cinnamon, the best foods to add it to, and why small daily habits often have the biggest long-term impact on your health. While cinnamon is not a cure or substitute for medical treatment, it can be an easy, inexpensive addition to a balanced diet and healthy lifestyle. Watch until the end to discover how one tiny change at breakfast may help you feel better, stay fuller longer, and start every morning on a healthier path. videos.title: Stop Chunking: The ‘Downhill’ Weight Shift
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Chunking: The ‘Downhill’ Weight Shift Click link in bio for lowest price on the Ball First Board. 
Now, the most important part of your weight shift is what I call going downhill. Imagine I'm on a, a giant mountain, a slope. I'm on a snowboard here, and I'm sliding downhill. I feel like I slide to the left. I get my weight over my lead side, specifically on the outside of my lead ankle.

My weight gets to the inside of my right foot, and then once I'm downhill here, I haven't started my downswing yet. My arms are still back in the, in the backswing position here. I get my weight left, then I come on through to the finish. You're gonna see that with every great player. That's one thing I designed in this Ball First Board. 

This is a brand-new training aid that I just released, just came out with. Spent over a year and a half designing this. These sticks are gonna tell you if I'm doing this downhill piece right, along with the foot pedal. So I'm gonna make my backswing here, and before I start my downswing, I'm gonna start going downhill.

There's two things I'll feel when I'm doing this correctly. Number one, I'm gonna feel like my weight gets over this lead stick,  so my, my outside of my lead leg actually bumps into this stick. That's good. That's what I should be feeling. And I'm gonna feel like the inside of my right foot is angled in, and I have this pedal to kinda help push me over there.

So I'm gonna feel like everything's going downhill. Once I get here, then I can turn on through to the finish. In a real golf swing, I'm gonna feel the weight transfer to the inside of this foot. A lot of times you'll hear people talk about rolling that foot in. That's exactly what I should be feeling like.

So backswing,  I'm gonna shift downhill.  This stick is in my lead leg. You see how my back foot is rolled in. The pressure's on the inside of my foot, and then from there, I'm gonna turn all the way on through to the finish. videos.title: Grok 5: The 6 Trillion Parameter Claim, Fact-Checked
videos.summary_source_text:
Grok 5: The 6 Trillion Parameter Claim, Fact-Checked Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
In this video: what xAI has officially confirmed about Grok 5, the rumored 6-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts architecture, how it stacks up against GPT-4o, Claude, and Llama 3 on real benchmark data, the multi-agent reasoning system, pricing predictions, and whether any of this gets xAI closer to AGI. We also cover the risks — including the MechaHitler incident and the 2026 Wired deepfake investigation — because no breakdown of Grok 5 is complete without them.

02:27 So When Does It Actually Launch?

videos.summary_source_text:
Varför är VW:s folkelbil bara för rika? VW har länge utlovat att ”demokratisera” elbilen och släppa en elbil vanligt folk har råd med. Nu kommer den... eller? 
Felix Björklund och Alrik Söderlind får sällskap av VW Sveriges chef Sten Forsberg. Han försvarar prissättningen, ger en känga åt politisk sävlighet och lanserar förslaget ”kommunala laddstolpar”

videos.title: My LAST EVER Open Qualifying?
videos.summary_source_text:
My LAST EVER Open Qualifying? Rick Shiels heads to Caldy Golf Club to take on regional Open Qualifying, the first step on the long road to The Open Championship. This is the real deal: one round, a tough field of aspiring professionals and amateurs, and only a handful of spots up for grabs to progress to Final Qualifying. Can Rick handle the nerves and post a score good enough to keep the Open dream alive?

Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:
https://www.youtube.com/@LIVGolf

LIMITED EDITION MERCH (UK & US)
My new limited edition merch is now available — thanks so much for the incredible support!
UK viewers: https://uk.rickshiels.com
US viewers: https://us.rickshiels.com

MORE FROM ME
🎙 Podcast — The Rick Shiels Golf Show
https://www.youtube.com/@RickShielsGolfShow
🏌️‍♂️Golf equipment reviews — H.I.T Golf
Honest, independent testing of the latest drivers, irons, putters, golf balls and tech.
https://www.youtube.com/@HITGolfReviews

OFFICIAL PARTNERS

Redvanly — premium golf apparel partners:
https://www.redvanly.com/pages/gift-guide?utm_source=rickshiels&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2025giftguide

FOLLOW ME ⬇️
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/RickShielsPGA/
X: https://twitter.com/RickShielsPGA
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/rickshielspga/
Website: http://www.rickshiels.com/

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Rick Shiels heads to Caldy Golf Club to take on regional Open Qualifying, the first step on the long road to The Open Championship. This is the real deal: one round, a tough field of aspiring professionals and amateurs, and only a handful of spots up for grabs to progress to Final Qualifying. videos.title: David Duchovny Lives the Technicolor Universe While Eating Spicy Wings | Hot Ones
videos.summary_source_text:
David Duchovny Lives the Technicolor Universe While Eating Spicy Wings | Hot Ones David Duchovny is a two-time Golden Globe winner who spent nine seasons chasing aliens as Fox Mulder in The X-Files and another seven playing a self-destructing novelist on Californication. He also has a shelf of published novels, a collection of poetry, and his fourth studio album as a musician, "Prince of Pieces," is set to release on August 13th. But how is he with spicy food? Find out as Duchovny takes on the wings of death and discusses the works of Samuel Beckett, Monster-of-the-Week nostalgia, and an MTV Rock N' Jock story for the ages.

videos.title: Almedalen 2026 - torsdag 25 juni
videos.summary_source_text:
Almedalen 2026 - torsdag 25 juni 8.15-9.00
Digital tillit och federativ infrastruktur – nyckeln till säker samverkan mellan aktörer
Offentlig sektor och näringsliv behöver i allt högre grad dela data och använda gemensamma tjänster. Hur förenklas detta genom digital tillit? Hur skapar vi en infrastruktur som möjliggör säker samverkan utan att varje aktör bygger egna lösningar?

9.30-10.15
När AI blir en kollega: Vem gör vad – och vem bär ansvaret?

AI har gått från att vara ett passivt verktyg till att bli en aktör som fattar beslut och driver arbetsflöden. Det ställer helt nya krav på hur ansvar fördelas, hur ledare leder och hur medarbetare samverkar med tekniken. Hur rustar vi organisationer för den förändringen – och vem bär ansvaret?

10.45-11.30 
Att följa pengarna – vad är bankernas roll i kampen mot välfärdsbrottsligheten?

Den kriminella ekonomin omsätter 352 miljarder kronor per år och välfärdsbrott är en växande del. Banker har en unik insyn i transaktioner, mönster och avvikelser som tidigt kan signalera missbruk av välfärdssystemen. Vad är bankernas roll i arbetet mot välfärdsbrottsligheten?

13.00-13.45 
Kan AI stärka demokratin och lösa samhällsproblem – på riktigt?

Kan AI användas för att sänka trösklar till demokratiskt deltagande, öka ungas valdeltagande och bygga förtroende för samhällets institutioner? Hur går vi från teknik och vision till att lösa stora samhällsutmaningar inom områden som klimat, hälsa, utbildning, inkludering och humanitärt arbete?

14.15-15.00 
Ett energisystem som håller – hur säkrar vi Sveriges framtida energiförsörjning?

Energibehoven ökar, och tillgång till säker och tillförlitlig energi till rimlig kostnad är avgörande för samhälle, företag och människor. Geopolitisk osäkerhet pressar systemet – men kan också påskynda omställningen. Så hur formar vi gemensamt en hållbar energiinfrastruktur för Sverige?

15.30-16.15 
Hur kan vi använda AI på ett säkert och förtroendeskapande sätt?

AI förändrar både offentlig och privat sektor i grunden – men hur hanterar vi riskerna när användningen ökar? Hur säkerställer vi att teknikutvecklingen går hand i hand med säkerhet, ansvar och efterlevnad av regler?

Läs mer här: https://www.pwc.se/sv/almedalen.html 8.15-9.00

videos.title: Shallow the Club in Transition
videos.summary_source_text: Shallow the Club in Transition Click link in bio for lowest price on the Blue Brick. I’m sharing one of my favorite drills to help you shallow the club in transition and keep the momentum moving the right way into the downswing. I add an alignment stick into the butt end of the club, let it slide down my right leg in the backswing to keep the clubhead out, then keep that same momentum through transition so the shaft shallows on plane. From there I point the butt end toward the ball, strike it, and keep rotating to the finish—making sure the right toe and foot pivot through. Then I use my Blue Brick set to the six iron vertical swing plane (the same angle pros use) to match the feel and get instant feedback by coming down parallel to the stick for the right amount of shallowing. #golf #golfswing #golfdrill #shallowing #downswing #transition #sixiron #ballstriking #golftraining #alignmentstick #bluebrick Click link in bio for lowest price on the Blue Brick. videos.title: Ringing in the Ears Is NOT an Ear Problem (Fix It in 7 Days)
videos.summary_source_text:
Ringing in the Ears Is NOT an Ear Problem (Fix It in 7 Days) Looking for tinnitus relief? Discover the root cause of ringing in the ears, how tinnitus may be connected to metabolic health, and what you can do to support ear health naturally in as little as 7 days.

0:00 How to stop ringing in ears
1:50 Testing for hearing problems
2:17 What is tinnitus? 
2:45 What causes ringing in ears?
3:38 Insulin resistance and tinnitus
4:31 Ringing in ears and blood sugar 
5:15 Fasting insulin test
6:11 Natural tinnitus relief
8:29 Tinnitus at night

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/3SulLfh

Learn more about blood sugar, nerve health, and other factors that could be affecting your hearing and overall health: 
▶️ https://youtu.be/OcMCBTDpDVU
▶️ https://youtu.be/yK14BDeKIpo
▶️ https://youtu.be/8OSlBn6qRqs

Check out this article to learn how to stop tinnitus in seconds: https://drbrg.co/4g0ao8E

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Ringing in the ears is often associated with past exposure to loud noise, but there may be additional factors involved.

Tinnitus is believed to originate in the brain rather than the ears themselves. When hearing loss occurs at a specific frequency, the brain may generate a sound to compensate for the missing signal.

Chronically elevated blood sugar can affect the tiny nerves and blood vessels of the inner ear. Many people have blood sugar problems for years without realizing it because insulin levels can rise long before blood sugar levels become noticeably abnormal.

Supporting healthy blood sugar levels may play an important role in tinnitus relief. Reducing carbohydrate intake and incorporating fasting may help support healthy insulin levels and improve nutrient delivery to the small blood vessels that support hearing.

These two nutrients help support nerve health and cardiovascular function for natural tinnitus relief:
1. Alpha-lipoic acid
2. Benfotiamine

Some people report improvements relatively quickly, while others may require more time depending on the underlying factors involved.

DATA:
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12574643
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12024736/
https://www.tinnitusjournal.com/articles/hyperinsulinemia-and-tinnitus-a-historical-cohort.pdf
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8897791/
https://bmjopen.bmj.com/content/15/3/e091507

If you're fighting your downswing, the real fix is probably in your backswing. Eric Cogorno and JT Thomas break down how to combine the correct shoulder turn with the correct hip rotation during the backswing to set up an easy, repeatable downswing. Once you get your hips moving correctly, the feeling is simple: they should never stop going. These golf drills give you simple feels and checkpoints so you stop fighting compensations on the way down and start fixing the cause. If you leave this video without trying the left side bend drill, you're leaving consistency on the table.

What You'll Learn:
• Why most downswing problems are actually caused by a flawed backswing
• The correct lead shoulder position: down and in toward the midline of the body
• The left side bend drill and how to feel it without using your hips
• Why a flat shoulder turn forces a steep, crashing downswing
• How the trail hip should rotate up and toward the target during the backswing
• The fist-width checkpoint for proper hip rotation
• Why your hips should never stop moving forward once they start
• The long lead shoulder drill for checking your backswing position at home

videos.title: Homey Stories — The Home That Opens the Door for Peter
videos.summary_source_text:
Homey Stories — The Home That Opens the Door for Peter Peter started using Homey as a hobby to automate a couple of garden lights. After a brain hemorrhage, he came back to it with a different question: could the same system help him stay in his own home?

Three years ago, Peter spent three weeks in a coma and lost his ability to speak. The Homey Pro he’d set up as a hobby became part of how he gets through the day. The curtains open by themselves, and he controls the lights and front door from his phone.

Rob from ROBBshop has known Peter for years and helped adapt his home. Peter’s Homey Story shows how a few simple Flows can make a real difference when everyday tasks get harder.

Read Peter’s story
  ↳ https://homey.app/en-us/blog/peters-accessible-smart-home/

videos.title: NEW Pagani Codalunga Speedster is the ULTIMATE Hypercar!
videos.summary_source_text:
NEW Pagani Codalunga Speedster is the ULTIMATE Hypercar! Mat is reviewing the all-new, ultra-rare Pagani Codalunga Speedster. He’ll walk you around the design, interior, engine & drivetrain, and find out how to fit the roof. 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-25-06

This is one of the rarest and most expensive cars Mat has ever reviewed! 

He’s flown over to Italy to visit Pagani, and he’s been allowed to get up close and personal with the new Pagani Codalunga Speedster! 

It’s ultra-rare, with only 10 to ever be sold! The question is, how does it differ from the Codalunga Coupe? And is it worth the €11,000,000 price tag?! Let’s take a closer look… 

Although the shape is still largely similar to the coupe, there are a few unique differences. Up top, there’s now a blade through the large air intake in the bumper, along with larger vents in the bonnet to help cool the radiator. Along the side, the gull-wing doors have been replaced with regular doors, which therefore means the wings in front and behind the doors are all-new. The huge air intake scoops behind the cabin are also more prominent. Around the back, there are loads of active aero components and ceramic-coated exhaust tips. What's more, unlike the Coupe edition, the speedster comes with six (although two are hidden lower down). Also, a special mention to this green paint - it looks absolutely magnificent! 

The interior of the Codlinga Speedster is the definition of refinement. The only materials you’ll find in here are metal, leather, wood, fabric or carbon - there isn’t one single piece of plastic! What’s more, the fabric on the doors and seats is so rare and expensive that it costs more than a regular family car! All buttons and switches just feel expensive when you touch them, and there’s a surprising amount of storage available. There is also bespoke luggage available, which, once again, is coated in the expensive fabric. There’s also one rare button which we haven’t seen on a car before - a fire extinguisher switch! 

The Codalunga Speedster uses the same engine as found in the Pagani Utopia. That means it’s powered by a 6-litre twin-turbo V12 that delivers 864hp and 1,10Nm. All this power is sent to the rear wheels via a 7-speed manual gearbox. 

So the question is - if you could, would you pay the eye-watering, pre-tax price of €11,000,000?! Stick with Mat to see if you should! Up top, there’s now a blade through the large air intake in the bumper, along with larger vents in the bonnet to help cool the radiator. Along the side, the gull-wing doors have been replaced with regular doors, which therefore means the wings in front and behind the doors are all-new. The huge air intake scoops behind the cabin are also more prominent. Around the back, there are loads of active aero components and ceramic-coated exhaust tips. What's more, unlike the Coupe edition, the speedster comes with six (although two are hidden lower down). Also, a special mention to this green paint - it looks absolutely magnificent! 

The interior of the Codlinga Speedster is the definition of refinement. The only materials you’ll find in here are metal, leather, wood, fabric or carbon - there isn’t one single piece of plastic! What’s more, the fabric on the doors and seats is so rare and expensive that it costs more than a regular family car! All buttons and switches just feel expensive when you touch them, and there’s a surprising amount of storage available. There is also bespoke luggage available, which, once again, is coated in the expensive fabric. There’s also one rare button which we haven’t seen on a car before - a fire extinguisher switch! 

The Codalunga Speedster uses the same engine as found in the Pagani Utopia. That means it’s powered by a 6-litre twin-turbo V12 that delivers 864hp and 1,10Nm. All this power is sent to the rear wheels via a 7-speed manual gearbox. 

So the question is - if you could, would you pay the eye-watering, pre-tax price of €11,000,000?! Stick with Mat to see if you should!
videos.summary: NEW Pagani Codalunga Speedster is the ULTIMATE Hypercar. Mat is reviewing the all-new, ultra-rare Pagani Codalunga Speedster.

videos.title: TRADE STOVE IS HOT! Carolina Hurricanes in Trade Rumors for Alexander Nikishin
videos.summary_source_text:
TRADE STOVE IS HOT! Carolina Hurricanes in Trade Rumors for Alexander Nikishin After a wild Tuesday of trades, it seems like the NHL is not slowing down as more trades went down on Wednesday. Despite the Washington Capitals getting Alex Tuch, the Carolina Hurricanes are still in a great spot. That said, they have cropped up in trade rumors regarding Alexander Nikishin. Does it mean anything? On the Thursday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the Nikishin trade rumor situation. Darren Dreger posted on X/Twitter that the Hurricanes are hearing interest from teams about the young Russian defenseman, who just won the Stanley Cup. Taking interest doesn't mean that they will trade him away. Does it make sense to move on from him? Secondly, the Hurricanes announced on Wednesday night that they extended Jusso Valimaki to a one-year, $900,000 deal to keep him in the organization after acquiring him last season from the Utah Mammoth. He played a big role for the Chicago Wolves last season in the AHL, en route to a Calder Cup Final appearance. What does it mean moving forward? Lastly, Zach concludes the show by sharing his thoughts on what could be an interesting offseason for the Hurricanes. With most of the forward core signed, outside of two guys on the defense, what could the Hurricanes do to prepare for next season? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: Two Tkachuks Unite in Florida and Byram Looks To Cash In Big With CHI | GS Off-Ice Special Edition
videos.summary_source_text:
Two Tkachuks Unite in Florida and Byram Looks To Cash In Big With CHI | GS Off-Ice Special Edition Welcome back to another offseason edition of GS Off Ice! 

Mark Robinson and Jesse Montano are back for another episode of league-wide hockey talk, and there is TONS to talk about after yesterdays BIZZARE slate of trades that went through.

From the news of Brady Tkachuk joining his brother Matthew in Florida, to Bowen Byram leaving the excitement of Buffalo to play with Connor Bedard in Chicago, the NHL news since the conclusion of the Stanley Cup has been plentiful, and we're breaking it all down.

We're also talking about the Toronto Maple Leafs BIGGEST priorirites for the off-season, and we're grateful to have NHL.com Toronto correspondent Dave McCarthy joining us to chat about the Maple Leafs.

Among all the crazy trades over the last few days, Buffalo has been one of the most active teams, especially after trading young star defenseman Bowen Byram. We've got WGRZ TV's Nate Mills joining us to weigh in on all the moves Buffalo has made after their exciting run in the playoffs, and if these moves are setting them up for continued success.

videos.title: Red Wings Dylan Larkin Reportedly Picks Dallas Stars As “Destination Of Choice” | DLLS Stars Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Red Wings Dylan Larkin Reportedly Picks Dallas Stars As “Destination Of Choice” | DLLS Stars Podcast Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

Sportsnet’s Nick Kypreos reported on Tuesday that Detroit Red Wings captain Dylan Larkin has selected the Dallas Stars as his preferred “destination of choice” after he requested a trade back at the beginning of June. Three weeks ago, we spent a good chunk of time talking about whether Larkin to Dallas made any sense and how it might work with the financials of the Stars current salary cap squeeze. Now that we are getting closer to the NHL Draft and Free Agency, Jason Robertson trade rumors are swirling and Larkin could be a replacement option. Also today, there was a bevy of trades on Tuesday and we will go through them all, keeping you up-to-date on all the latest moves around the NHL.

videos.title: Jack Leiter's Ankle Surgery Leaves Texas Rangers With A Rotation Issue | DLLS Rangers
videos.summary_source_text:
Jack Leiter's Ankle Surgery Leaves Texas Rangers With A Rotation Issue | DLLS Rangers Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

An ALLCITY Network Production

The Texas Rangers are in scramble mode with their starting rotation after right-hander Jack Leiter underwent surgery Tuesday to remove a loose body from his right ankle. The team didn’t offer a timeline for his return, but he could be out until August. In the meantime, the Rangers will search for answers to fill Leiter’s spot. There are multiple internal candidates, beginning with Cal Quantrill and Joe Ross. The season won’t stop for them. They finished a three-game series at Miami on Wednesday and still have seven games to go on their road trip. Next up: a four-game series at Toronto, whose fans are stuffing the All-Star Game ballot box. It could cost Ezequiel Duran his first All-Star appearance.

videos.title: 24.06.26 - RIFT Training
videos.summary_source_text:
24.06.26 - RIFT Training RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

videos.title: Jag intervjuade den smarta Kina-bilroboten – tills hon blev sur på mig!
videos.summary_source_text:
Jag intervjuade den smarta Kina-bilroboten – tills hon blev sur på mig! Här kommer min första personliga intervju med en Robot!

Cherys bilrobot Mornine är till salu – och hon vet väldigt mycket. Som hur tysk bilindustri bäst borde möta den Kinesiska, varför Kinesiska bilar är billigare än tyska - även om båda utvecklats och tillverkas i kina, vilka Cherys största utmaningar är i Sverige, hur AI påverkar bilutvecklingen, när man kommer tjäna pengar i Sverige, vilka Cherys och VW:s styrkor och svagheter är. 
Och så vidare.

Men hon har också lite koll på vad lycka är, hur bilarbetarna mår och varför födelsetalen är låga. Men när demokrati kommer på tal blir hon mycket tyst och i slutet av intervjun blir hon riktigt trött på mig och mina frågor, och knuffas och använder olika tekniker för att får slut på det hela. 

Kan en robot ha känslor? Mornine är i alla fall inte iskallt nollställd, utan blir både glad, sur, speedad , växlar språk och blir sarkastisk!

Vad hon verkligen vill tala om är självklart Cherys bilar och deras avancerade teknik! Så det talar vi också om. Den sista frågan blev: "Vad tycker du om mina frågor?"   

Gissa vad hon svarade?

Se till att prenumerera så du inte missar något!

videos.title: Giannis to Miami SHOCKS NBA + Are the Denver Nuggets Preparing a BLOCKBUSTER Trade? Morning LIV(E)
videos.summary_source_text:
Giannis to Miami SHOCKS NBA + Are the Denver Nuggets Preparing a BLOCKBUSTER Trade? Morning LIV(E) The NBA offseason just delivered its first massive blockbuster as Giannis Antetokounmpo heads to the Miami Heat! Liv breaks down what the Giannis trade means for both Miami and Milwaukee, whether the Heat are now legitimate championship contenders, and what the future holds for the Bucks in a post-Giannis era.

Plus, the Denver Nuggets made a surprising move on draft night by trading out of the first round. Is Denver quietly stockpiling assets for a blockbuster trade of their own? Liv discusses the Nuggets' draft strategy, rumors surrounding Jaylen Brown, potential trade pieces including Aaron Gordon and Christian Braun, and whether Denver is preparing to make a major splash around Nikola Jokić this offseason.

videos.title: Avalanche Make Another Trade With Nashville and Send Jack Drury to the Predators
videos.summary_source_text:
Avalanche Make Another Trade With Nashville and Send Jack Drury to the Predators The Colorado Avalanche shake up their roster, sending Jack Drury to the Nashville Predators for Fedor Svechkov and Zachary L’Heureux. Did the Avs sacrifice too much depth for cap space, or will these young forwards pay off under the leadership of Nathan MacKinnon and Gabriel Landeskog?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down the trade’s implications, questioning LaRue’s reputation for suspensions and Svechkov’s faceoff struggles. The conversation explores the financial strategy behind the move, Nashville fans’ reactions, and what losing an elite fourth-line center means for Colorado’s Stanley Cup hopes. Additional topics include major league trades—highlighting Alex Tuch’s blockbuster deal to the Washington Capitals—and the escalating market for defensemen like Bo Byram. Don’t miss sharp analysis of Martin Nečas’ breakout 100-point season and what the Avs must do to stay contenders in the evolving NHL landscape.

videos.title: I Accidentally Built an AI Coding Startup
videos.summary_source_text:
I Accidentally Built an AI Coding Startup 🧞‍♂️ Join Creator Magic here: https://mrc.fm/cmc

I set out to fix one small problem with Claude Code and somehow ended up building a startup. In this live build I walk you through Tank, the AI agent orchestrator my community accidentally turned into a real product, and I set the whole thing up on camera: the founding team, Slack, Claude Team, the GitHub repos and the roadmap.

Watch me go from a side project to a company in real time, messy bits and all. If you want the tools, the templates and the community behind it, come in here: https://mrc.fm/cmc

What I cover:

- The accidental startup story behind Tank
- Bringing on the founding team and setting up access
- The open core plan and where Tank goes next
- Building live with Claude Code and AI agents

New here? I am Mike from Creator Magic. I build AI agents, automations and self hosted tools with Claude Code and share all of it as I go.

videos.title: 3 Different Shots to Play From the Bunker #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
3 Different Shots to Play From the Bunker #golfswing #golftips Most golfers can get the ball out of the bunker — but not with the control they want.
This video shows you three bunker shot levels: get it out, get it controlled, and get it spinning.
It all comes down to your entry point — where the club first enters the sand.
Start experimenting with how close to the ball you strike the sand to control spin, rollout, and stopping power.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Replaced Claude Code With New Claude Tag
videos.summary_source_text:
Anthropic Just Replaced Claude Code With New Claude Tag Anthropic just dropped Claude Tag, a new team version of Claude Code that works inside Slack, connects to company tools, and keeps tasks moving in public channels. But the bigger story is what came next: hidden AI reasoning blocks, encrypted thinking logs, replay risks, Japan’s new Fugu router model, and OpenAI’s new research on making alignment transfer across domains.

👉 Publish your expert AI skill as a paid agent app on Capafy: https://capafy.ai/

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com

📌 What You’ll See:
Anthropic launches Claude Tag in Slack with plans for wider rollout
SOURCE: https://www.reuters.com/technology/anthropic-launches-claude-tag-research-preview-slack-users-2026-06-23/

Claude Tag replaces the old Claude Slack chatbot for team workflows
SOURCE: https://m.economictimes.com/tech/artificial-intelligence/anthropic-launches-claude-tag-everything-you-need-to-know-about-the-slack-native-ai-agent/articleshow/131958086.cms

Matt Green exposes encrypted reasoning blocks in ChatGPT and Claude
SOURCE: https://blog.cryptographyengineering.com/2026/05/29/fooling-around-with-encrypted-reasoning-blobs/

Sakana AI launches Fugu as a router for Claude, GPT, and Gemini
SOURCE: https://sakana.ai/fugu/

OpenAI publishes new research on alignment that transfers across domains
SOURCE: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24014

🚨 Why It Matters
This is bigger than Anthropic launching another Claude product. AI is moving from private chat windows into real company workflows, while the hidden reasoning behind these systems is becoming a trust, security, and control problem. The next AI race is about who controls the agents inside teams.

videos.title: IQ + Mechanics = Unstoppable Shot
videos.summary_source_text:
IQ + Mechanics = Unstoppable Shot The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: 9 Free AI Skills That Feel Like Cheat Codes
videos.summary_source_text:
9 Free AI Skills That Feel Like Cheat Codes 9 free skills and plug-ins that make Claude Code, Codex, and other AI coding agents dramatically more useful, and yes, all of them are free.
Skills and plug-ins are reusable instruction files that give your AI agent a repeatable behavior instead of starting from scratch every time. In this video I break down 9 of my favorites, install them live, and put several of them head to head so you can see exactly what changes.

I cover GStack (turns Claude Code into a full virtual engineering team), Stop Slop (strips AI tells out of your writing), Graphify (turns any codebase or knowledge base into a queryable graph your agent can use as memory), Understand Anything (visual onboarding maps for any codebase), Last 30 Days (real time sentiment research across Reddit, X, YouTube, and more), Anthropic's Front End Design skill and the Taste skill (head to head design tests), and ReMotion vs HyperFrames (AI generated animations from a single prompt).

Every skill works across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenClaw, Hermes, VS Code, and GitHub Copilot. Links to every single one below.

Discover More:
🛠️ Explore AI Tools & News: https://futuretools.io/
📰 Weekly Newsletter: https://futuretools.io/newsletter

Socials:
❌ Twiter/X: https://x.com/mreflow
🖼️ Instagram: https://instagram.com/mr.eflow
🧵 Threads: https://www.threads.net/@mr.eflow
🟦 LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/matt-wolfe-30841712/
👍 Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/mattrwolfe

Skills and plug-ins mentioned:
GStack: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack
Stop Slop: https://github.com/hardikpandya/stop-slop
Graphify: https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify
Understand Anything: https://github.com/Egonex-AI/Understand-Anything
Last 30 Days: https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill
Front End Design (Anthropic): https://www.skills.sh/anthropics/skills/frontend-design
Taste Skill: https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill
ReMotion: https://github.com/remotion-dev/remotion
HyperFrames: https://github.com/heygen-com/hyperframes

If you want more breakdowns like this, let me know in the comments and I'll keep them coming.

Let’s work together!
- Brand, sponsorship & business inquiries: mattwolfe@smoothmedia.co

videos.title: 20 Second Form Fix: DB Bench Press
videos.summary_source_text:
20 Second Form Fix: DB Bench Press If your elbows flare straight out to the sides when you press, you are likely putting your shoulders in a worse position and taking tension off the chest. Instead, think about the dumbbells following the sides of a triangle: wider at the bottom, slightly in at the top.

Aim for about a 45 degree angle with your arms. This will protect your shoulders in the process and keep them healthy so that you can keep bench pressing over time.

At the bottom of each rep, you want to target the touch point. Bring the dumbbells down toward the outer line of your lower chest. This will make sure you are getting low enough to put stretch tension on the pecs at the bottom of the range of motion.

Follow this quick form fix so can you keep the press stronger, safer, and more effective for building your chest.

For more exercise how-tos and exercise form fixes, be sure to stay tuned to this channel and remember to subscribe so that you never miss another video from a physical therapist with a pro sports background as a PT and strength coach.

videos.title: URINATING TOO OFTEN?  HERE'S WHEN TO WORRY | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
URINATING TOO OFTEN?  HERE'S WHEN TO WORRY | Dr. Mandell Are you urinating more often than usual? Waking up multiple times at night to use the bathroom? Constantly looking for the nearest restroom? Frequent urination can be caused by many different factors, including an enlarged prostate, elevated blood sugar, overactive bladder, urinary tract infections, medications, caffeine, alcohol, stress, anxiety, and other underlying health conditions. In this video, I'll explain what's considered normal, when you should be concerned, warning signs you should never ignore, and practical steps you can take to help reduce urinary frequency. You'll also learn how keeping a simple bladder diary, limiting bladder irritants, practicing bladder training, strengthening your pelvic floor muscles, and making a few lifestyle changes may help improve symptoms. If you've ever wondered whether your frequent urination is something to worry about, this video is for you.

*********************************

videos.title: How to Judge Pace on Downhill Putts  #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
How to Judge Pace on Downhill Putts  #golfswing #golftips Downhill putts are easy to overhit because gravity does most of the work. By focusing on playing the putt with just enough pace and letting the slope do the rest, you’ll improve distance control. This simple system helps you judge speed more accurately and avoid the long comeback putt. The goal is always the same — give yourself the best chance to avoid a three-putt.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. Downhill putts are easy to overhit because gravity does most of the work. So I dug through the roadmaps, system cards, and actual benchmark numbers to find out if GPT-6 or Grok-5 is actually closer — and the honest answer isn't the one either fanbase wants.

videos.title: The 2 Keys (Spine Tilt + Momentum)
videos.summary_source_text:
The 2 Keys (Spine Tilt + Momentum) Click link in bio for lowest price on the Ball First Board. 
I explain the two essentials to shifting your weight like PGA Tour pros: spine tilt toward the target at impact and forward momentum as the lower body moves toward the target in transition. After studying 120 PGA Tour players, I found they all combine these two, while most amateurs only do one—either staying on the backside and scooping/casting, or overloading the lead side and then falling back during release. I use a throwing analogy to show why momentum forward prevents you from falling back and helps you strike ball-first. I demonstrate how to keep the upper body behind the ball (avoiding a reverse pivot), control hip movement on the backswing, and then shift to the lead side so you’re stable at impact for consistent, high-quality contact, and I mention the Ball First board as a training aid. Click link in bio for lowest price on the Ball First Board.

videos.title: Adam Richman Eats 126-Year-Old Texas BBQ! | Pro Moves
videos.summary_source_text:
Adam Richman Eats 126-Year-Old Texas BBQ! | Pro Moves Central Texas barbecue is the stuff of legend. Over the course of the next three episodes, host Adam Richman will be taking you on an epic journey through some of Hill Country's most distinct and celebrated barbecue establishments. The adventure begins today at Kreuz Market, the second oldest barbecue joint in the Lone Star State, and the crown jewel of Lockhart, the official "Barbecue Capital of Texas" as designated by the state legislature. Along for the ride is Texas Monthly BBQ editor Daniel Vaughn, who will be giving Adam all of the tips and tricks he needs to know to get the most out of his Kreuz experience. Come for the beef ribs and brisket, but stay for a heartwarming interview with legendary Kreuz Market pitmaster Roy Perez.

videos.title: GPT-6 Cyber Explained: Trillions of Parameters, 2M Context, Built to Hack
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT-6 Cyber Explained: Trillions of Parameters, 2M Context, Built to Hack Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
OpenAI is reportedly building GPT-6 Cyber — a cybersecurity-focused AI model trained to scan code for vulnerabilities, chain weaknesses into real exploits, and even write proof-of-concept attack code. Unlike standard models built to refuse dangerous requests, a Cyber model is built to engage with them. We break down what's confirmed, what's rumored, and why OpenAI is reportedly locking this one behind identity-verified, vetted access instead of releasing it to the public.

Chapters:
00:00 Intro
01:13 First, A Reality Check
02:04 What "Cyber" Actually Means
03:07 The GPT-6 Base It's Built On
04:25 What A GPT-6 Cyber Would Actually Do
05:25 Why OpenAI Is Locking It Down
06:42 The Safety Story, And Where It's Thin
07:44 How Much Of This Is Even Confirmed
08:48 Who This Is Actually For
09:41 The Risks Worth Actually Worrying About


🔔 Subscribe for skepticism-first breakdowns of every major AI release.

💬 Drop a comment: do you think gating a model like GPT-6 Cyber actually keeps it contained, or does powerful capability always leak eventually?

videos.title: I Dropped My Ego And Played My BEST Golf!
videos.summary_source_text:
I Dropped My Ego And Played My BEST Golf! Do forgiving golf clubs actually make you play better?

Guy takes the most forgiving clubs on the market onto the golf course for 9 holes to find out. From drivers to irons, we're putting forgiveness to the test in a real-world round to see if these clubs can save bad shots, lower scores, and help everyday golfers play better golf.

Can technology really make that much difference? Let's find out.


Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: Volvo XC70 provkörd i Kina!
videos.summary_source_text:
Volvo XC70 provkörd i Kina! I Kina säljs en Volvoversion av Lynk & Co 08, som är märkets storsäljare: XC70.
Det har sagts att den ska exporteras till 70 marknader. Men det var några år sedan det sades. Gäller det fortfarande?
Är den bättre än XC60? Eller till och med EX60?
Borde Volvo ta in den till Sverige? 
Än så länge är inte Kinesiska laddhybrider belagda med extra strafftullar, som elbilarna. Men det finns ett EU-förslag om just detta.
Jag har kört nya XC70 i Sanya, som är Kinas "Hawaii" och har mina egna teorier om hur Volvo ska kunna komma tillbaka på den Kinesiska marknaden.

Se till att prenumerera så du inte missar något!

Se filmerna utan reklam före alla andra: 
Bli gärna medlem och stöd kanalen, som blir reklamfri! 29 kr/månad
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCU-4UJDGqQWK5Iv4wXDlzGQ/join

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

videos.title: The Wildest Things People Vibe Coded With Fable 5
videos.summary_source_text:
The Wildest Things People Vibe Coded With Fable 5 I messed up. 

Everyone has been talking about the drama that went down around Claude Fable and Mythos getting shut down, including myself. But I got distracted from the REAL story here, which is just how crazy good these models were and the amazing things people were able to build with them.  

These are just a few examples, but it's insane that people built full scale projects and business ideas and NONE of them were engineers with massive dev budgets. They were just regular people with a good idea. 

What used to require a full development team and months of work now takes a single afternoon and a good AI model. We are watching in real time a massive shift in WHO gets to build things. 

But of course, the party was cut short because Anthropic was ordered to suspend foreign access to Fable 5 over security concerns, literally describing their own tech as "too powerful." 

And I get why that sounds terrifying. But look at what people actually did with it in the short window they had: they solved problems, built businesses, and created cool things to share with the world. That’s what happens when you put genuinely useful tech into the hands of the people. 

#Claude #Anthropic #AI #Entrepreneurship #vibecoding I messed up.

What used to require a full development team and months of work now takes a single afternoon and a good AI model. We are watching in real time a massive shift in WHO gets to build things. 

But of course, the party was cut short because Anthropic was ordered to suspend foreign access to Fable 5 over security concerns, literally describing their own tech as "too powerful." 

And I get why that sounds terrifying. But look at what people actually did with it in the short window they had: they solved problems, built businesses, and created cool things to share with the world. That’s what happens when you put genuinely useful tech into the hands of the people.

videos.title: 11 Warning Signs Your Body Is Begging for B12
videos.summary_source_text:
11 Warning Signs Your Body Is Begging for B12 Your body’s begging for vitamin B12 if you have any of these symptoms:

• Depression, bipolar, or schizophrenia
• Memory lapses and brain fog
• Loss of smell or taste
• Burning mouth or tongue
• Sudden blurry vision
• Balance problems
• Tingling or numbness in the hands and feet
• Weakness in the legs
• Smooth, shiny tongue
• Pale skin
• Racing heart

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4oo64Cr

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon—search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

B12 deficiency can mimic serious neurological conditions, and many people don’t realize they have a problem. While B12 is found in foods like eggs, liver, meat, and fish, some people may need additional support. Hydroxocobalamin is considered one of the best forms because it’s the natural form the body recognizes and uses efficiently.

videos.title: Trying Harder Isn’t Helping Your Body Change
videos.summary_source_text:
Trying Harder Isn’t Helping Your Body Change Have you been trying hard to change your body, but still feel stuck?

Many people approach pain, tension, posture, and movement problems through more effort — more stretching, more strengthening, more correction, more discipline.

But sometimes trying harder is not what helps the body change.

Sometimes more effort creates more pressure, and the body responds by protecting even more.

In this video, I share why your body may not need more effort — it may need more permission. You’ll also experience a short somatic movement exploration to sense how less effort can create a very different experience.

If you’d like to explore this approach more deeply, try my free guided somatic movement session here: https://taro-iwamoto.thinkific.com/courses/masterclass

If you feel ready for deeper, more structured support, you can learn more about my course here:
https://taroiwamoto.com/thecourse

About Taro Iwamoto
I help self-aware adults who are exhausted by recurring pain, tension, and fear cycles despite everything they’ve tried, and who are ready to stop chasing fixes and follow a deeper somatic, nervous-system-based path to lasting change, so they can trust their body again and fully live their life with ease, freedom, and confidence.

videos.title: The #1 Most Dangerous Ingredient (Up to 600 Days to Remove From Your Body)
videos.summary_source_text:
The #1 Most Dangerous Ingredient (Up to 600 Days to Remove From Your Body) Are seed oils bad for your health? Discover the truth about seed oils, linoleic acid, omega-6 fats, and how changes in the modern diet may be affecting your heart, cellular health, and overall well-being.

0:00 Seed oils explained 
0:51 Linoleic acid
2:10 Oxidized oils 
3:15 What seed oils do to your body
3:55 Seed oil controversy
4:52 Seed oil dangers 
5:35 Cholesterol myths
7:10 Omega-6 fats and omega-3 fatty acids
8:12 Eliminating seed oils 

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/3QEEnIP

Learn more about healthy fats, omega-3 fatty acids, and the hidden effects of modern cooking oils:
▶️ https://youtu.be/NOV5vIsjQhg
▶️ https://youtu.be/8iA49h1HpWA
▶️ https://youtu.be/oQ4--K5kWLQ

Read this article to learn which seed oils to avoid, and what to use instead: https://drbrg.co/4w5tDlZ

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Fat tissue is more than just stored energy; it’s living tissue built from the foods you eat. One of the primary fats found in seed oils is linoleic acid, an omega-6 fatty acid.

Over the past several decades, the amount of linoleic acid stored in the body has increased dramatically. Some researchers estimate that the average body fat contained about 6% linoleic acid in the 1960s, compared to roughly 18% today. Linoleic acid levels in breast milk have also increased significantly during the same period, while soybean oil consumption has risen substantially.

One concern with seed oils is that linoleic acid can oxidize when exposed to heat, especially during repeated heating and reheating. This process can create toxic byproducts that may negatively affect health.

Cholesterol is commonly used as a marker of cardiovascular health, but the body naturally produces cholesterol, and some researchers question whether it tells the whole story.
Another important consideration is the balance between omega-6 and omega-3 fatty acids. 

While a balanced intake is important, many people consume significantly more omega-6 fats than omega-3s.

To improve your omega fatty acid balance, focus on reducing highly processed seed oils and increasing omega-3-rich foods such as fatty fish, fish oil, and cod liver oil.

DATA:
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4836695/
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11849496/ 
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0002916523233031 
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6860741/ 
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4908872/

videos.title: Stop Popping Up in Your Downswing
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Popping Up in Your Downswing Click link in bio for lowest price on the Ball First Board. 
Most players don’t realize how weight shifts affect posture: when your weight drifts to your toes, you extend and pop up to avoid falling forward, and when it drifts to your heels, you naturally bend into posture to avoid falling backward. In the golf swing, many players lose posture in the downswing because they move toward their toes. I use the Ball First Board with an alignment stick behind me to create the feel of pushing my hips back, keeping pressure into the stick, and keeping my weight in the inside heels—especially through the transition. That heel-pressure and “rear end back” feel helps me stay in posture like the pros all the way to the finish. If you want a Ball First Board, check the link around the video or in my bio—stock is limited, and if it’s sold out, join the wait list. Click link in bio for lowest price on the Ball First Board.

videos.title: DRAFTING SEASON! Who Could the Carolina Hurricanes Take at 31st Overall?
videos.summary_source_text:
DRAFTING SEASON! Who Could the Carolina Hurricanes Take at 31st Overall? As we get closer to the 2026 NHL Entry Draft, the Carolina Hurricanes have the 31st overall pick. While most of the time they trade out of the first round to get more draft capital, what if they kept the pick? That said, if they do, who do they take at 31? On Tuesday, we talked about Tommy Bleyl; who else could be solid at 31? On the Wednesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on why center Maddox Dagenais of the Québec Remparts might be a solid option at 31. What did he do in 2025-26 that put himself into a good spot to get picked in the later part of Round 1? What could he bring to the Hurricanes? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on how JP Hurlbert of the Kamloops Blazers is a good option as well. What does the right winger bring to the organization that has a good amount of top wingers already? Finally, there were a plethora of trades in the NHL on June 23. What does it mean for the Hurricanes as the league is trading people away more than we saw at the trade deadline in March? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes. 

 

 

 

Follow & Subscribe on all Podcast platforms: 

🎧 https://link.chtbl.com/LOCarolinaHurricanes?sid=YouTube 

Locked On NHL League-Wide: Every Team, Fantasy, Prospects & More: 

🎧 https://linktr.ee/LockedOnNHL 

 

 

Everydayer Club

#carolinahurricanes #carolinaculture #soundthesiren #nhl As we get closer to the 2026 NHL Entry Draft, the Carolina Hurricanes have the 31st overall pick. While most of the time they trade out of the first round to get more draft capital, what if they kept the pick? That said, if they do, who do they take at 31? What wedge grinds do YOU need 🤔 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: Understand and learn the circle of speed, part 2: Easy drills.
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
Understand and learn the circle of speed, part 2: Easy drills. Two easy drills to feel the proper path of the club and easily see if we do it correct or wrong. #golfswing #golf #golftips 

0:00 Intro to how to understand and learn the circle of speed

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 Two easy drills to feel the proper path of the club and easily see if we do it correct or wrong. #golfswing #golf #golftips

videos.title: Salladen du behöver i sommar! Pappa Poons Asiatiska sommarsallad recept i beskrivningen
videos.summary_source_text:
Salladen du behöver i sommar! Pappa Poons Asiatiska sommarsallad recept i beskrivningen Pappa Poons Asiatiska Sommar Sallad, en sallad du sent glömmer, frisk, fräch och krämig! Kan det vara årets sallad?
Recept:
4 portioner
 ca 40 minuter

Ingredienser
Kyckling
 800 g kycklinglårfilé gärna med skin på
 2 msk japansk soja
 1 msk fisksås
 1 msk socker
 2 vitlöksklyftor, rivna



Picklade grönsaker
 4 morötter, tunt strimlade
 ½  rättika, tunt strimlad
 1 dl risvinäger
 1 dl vatten
 0,5 dl socker
 1 tsk salt

Krutonger


Sallad
 1 litet huvud romansallad eller gemsallad
 1 gurka, skivad
 2 salladslökar, skivade
 1 näve färsk mynta
 2dl krutonger 

Dressing
 2dl majonnäs
 1 msk sriracha
 1 tsk sesamolja
 1 msk limejuice
3 msk japansk sojasås
2 msk fisksås
1 msk  sirap

Gör så här
Blanda risvinäger, vatten, socker och salt tills sockret löst sig och lägg ner morot och rättika låt stå och pickla medan resten lagas
Blanda soja, fisksås,socker, vitlök och olja och vänd runt kycklingen i marinaden, Rosta kycklingen i ugnen på 200 °c i ca 25-30 min, låt vila och skär sedan i skivor.
Blanda risvinäger, vatten, socker och salt tills sockret löst sig och lägg ner morot och rättika låt stå och pickla medan resten lagas
Lägg sallad, gurka, salladslök, örter och krutonger i en stor skål eller på ett stort fat
Toppa med den varma kycklingen och de picklade grönsakerna
Ringla över dressingen precis innan servering så krutongerna fortfarande behåller lite crunch

videos.title: Lambo Huracan Perf v Zeekr 8X: DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
Lambo Huracan Perf v Zeekr 8X: DRAG RACE We’re drag racing the Chinese Zeekr 8X against a Lamborghini Huracan Performante over the 1/4-mile and 1/2 mile. We’ll also test the two against each other in a brake test from 100mph.

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-160626

It’s time for a China vs Italy drag race! 

We’ve got our hands on the Zeekr 8X SUV, and it’s going up against the infamous Lamborghini Huracan Performante! 

The question is, can an SUV really come close to beating a sports car over the 1/4-mile?! Let’s take a closer look at the stats. 

Starting with the Lamborghini, it’s powered by a 5.2-litre naturally-aspirated V10 that delivers 640hp and 600Nn. This power is sent to all four wheels via a dual-clutch automatic gearbox, and it weighs in at just 1482kg. As of today, it’s worth around £180,000. 

Then we come to the Zeekr. Under the bonnet sits a 2-litre turbocharged petrol engine… But this is used to power three electric motors! These combine to produce an incredible 1,400hp and 1,410Nm, with this power sent to all four wheels. It’s incredibly heavy, though, tipping the scales at 2,820kg! However, it’s much cheaper than the Lambo, costing just £54,300!
